column5: Chapter Title (Mandatory)
column7: Question ID (Mandatory and Unique, for example: 1.1, 1.2, Etc)
column10: Answer Type (Mandatory, one of the following options: 1 for Open Answer, 2 for Dropdown - Single Select, 3 for Dropdown - Single Select - Open answer, 4 for Date, 5 for Dropdown - Multiple Select, 6 for Dropdown - Multiple Select - Open answer)
column11: Dropdown Options (Mandatory if column G is Dropdown. List options for the dropdown separated by a pipe character, for example: Yes|No|Undefined)
column12: DropDown Warnings (Optional, for every option on column H you can define a message, for example: Great|Oh|Think twice)
column13: Dropdown Scoring (Mandatory, set this to 1 if you want to enable scoring for this question. Otherwise set to 0)
column14: Dropdown Option Score (Mandatory if J is set to 1, set the score of the question as an integer positive number, for example 10)
column15: Dropdown Option Score Multiplier (Mandatory if J is set to 1. For every option of the dropdown in column H define an integer positive number that will be multiplied by the value on column K, for example: 1|2|10)
column16: Dropdown Option Show another Question (Optional and only possible if column G is dropdown.For every option in column H you can define one or more question IDs from column D to be shown when the option is selected. For example: 1.2 1.6||6.7 would display questions 1.2 and 1.6 if the first option is selected, none if the second is selected and 6.7 if the third option is selected)
column17: Question Comments & Attachments (Mandatory, select 0 if you do not want to show this option or 1 if you want to let users provide comments and attachments for each question)

This policy applies to:
- All production and non-production systems, applications, infrastructure, and services.
- All changes that may impact availability, performance, security, or integrity of systems.
- All capacity-related activities affecting systems, networks, and services.
- All personnel involved in operational management, system administration, and service delivery.

column3: - Management: Ensures operational management activities are governed, resourced, and aligned with business needs. - The Information Security function: Defines security requirements related to operational changes and capacity planning, with the support of other department managers. - IT / Operations Teams: Plan, implement, and manage changes and capacity in accordance with this policy. - System and Service Owners: Approve changes affecting their systems and ensure capacity meets business requirements. - Suppliers and Service Providers: Comply with operational management requirements defined in contracts and agreements. ---

column3: - Operational changes must be planned, assessed, approved, and documented. - Changes must not introduce unmanaged security, availability, or performance risks. - System and service capacity must be monitored and managed proactively. - Capacity limitations must be identified and addressed before they impact operations. - Operational management activities must be repeatable, controlled, and auditable. ---

column3: - All changes to systems, applications, infrastructure, and configurations must follow a defined change management process. - Changes must be: - Classified (e.g. standard, normal, emergency). - Assessed for impact on availability, performance, and security. - Approved before implementation. - Change implementation must: - Be planned and scheduled. - Include rollback or recovery considerations. - Avoid unnecessary disruption to business operations. - Emergency changes must: - Be justified and documented. - Be reviewed retrospectively. - Changes must be tested where feasible prior to deployment. - Change records must be maintained.
